fix.description: Built the full-screen collectable card. Tap any Personal-best card → it opens like a trading card (screenshot): orange gradient art zone with a TROPHY (top-left) + strength emblem + a slow holographic foil shimmer, over a clean stat panel — big weight hero (140kg), est. 1RM chip (~163kg), reps + date. Flip-in animation, tap-outside / X / Esc to close, reduced-motion safe. This is the 'pokemon cards / nice full screen ui + trophies' part. The 'attached videos' half is reserved by the 'Add a clip' button — that's a separate next step (it needs video upload + storage + playback; quick Q when you're ready: should the clip be the user recording/uploading their own PR lift, stored on our backend? I'll build it that way unless you'd rather link an external URL). Frontend 1de68415, visible after the next app release.

previousFix.description: Ran it live on /activity (your reply: 'run it and give screenshot'). The flat repeated 'best 20kg x10' PR list is now the swipeable 'Personal bests' carousel: trophy header + '6 this month' pill, one card per lift with the weight as the hero (Conventional Deadlift 140kg, Barbell Back Squat 120kg, ...), reps + how-long-ago underneath, third card peeking to signal horizontal swipe. Sits cleanly above Today's Workout / Pull Day A. Calm/tier-free per the design guardrails. Screenshot attached is the actual running build (commit 421c82dd).

issues.description: When the build activity plan open the activity page directly with prefilled current workout in the entire page

fix.description: Built it per your note ('don't build the plan again but have the existing plan for exercise'). 'Build Activity Plan' no longer dumps you into the full 7-day workout builder. It now opens a focused Daily Activity editor (shot 1) PREFILLED from your existing plan (here 11,500 steps, 4x cardio) — tap to adjust steps/cardio/NEAT, then 'Save & view activity' saves ONLY the targets and drops you on the Activity page (shot 2: Steps 0/11,500). Your workout stays exactly as it was (still '0 of 6 exercises' — no rebuild). New backend PUT /onboarding/custom-plan/activity-targets upserts the targets onto your active plan; nothing touches the workout days. Backend 4793d3e + frontend 7b916b05, both deployed/pushed; verified end-to-end (target round-trips and the plan's 6 workout days are untouched). Visible after the next app release.

issues.reviewReply: yes build it and make sure we don't build the plan again but have the existing plan for exercise

issues.description: Calories overshoot then orignal proposed plan
issues.reviewReason: Dug into this with prod data. The diet plan has no stored 'target' — the kcal shown (2991) is the SUM of the assembled meals. On the test account the pipeline is CORRECT: computed target 1824 == assembled meals 1824 (TDEE 2189 minus deficit, floored at BMR), so I can't reproduce a 2991-style overshoot there. Most likely cause: the build wizard's PROPOSED target comes from the client calc (calcOnboardingStats) which has a goal-completion-DATE trajectory override (a steeper deficit to hit your target date), but the SAVED/displayed plan uses the server calc (calculate_target_calories) which is deficit-only with NO date logic. If a date was set, the proposed (lower) and the saved (higher) legitimately diverge -> looks like an overshoot. The builder also tolerates up to +10% over target before blocking. To fix the RIGHT one safely (this calc drives everyone's calorie goals) I need: the affected account's email, OR did you set a goal completion date on that build, OR the proposed number you saw vs the 2991? Once I know which target should win (date-driven trajectory or standard deficit), I'll reconcile the two calcs so the proposed and the saved plan always match.
issues.reviewReply: it was different during the onboarding

issues.reviewReason: Diagnosed — this is a Google Cloud Console config issue, not a code bug (so it needs you, I can't access the console). What I verified is CORRECT: the sign-in code (src/lib/google-auth.ts + backend verify_google_token) is fine, and the frontend client ID (VITE_GOOGLE_WEB_CLIENT_ID) matches the backend GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID (both 415757416413-d8k…). Since the web/token path is configured right, 'Google sign-in didn't complete' in the RELEASED Android app is almost certainly the app's signing-key SHA-256 not being registered on the Android OAuth client. Fix: Google Cloud Console → APIs & Services → Credentials → the OAuth 2.0 'Android' client for package com.nyus.nyusand → add BOTH SHA-256 fingerprints: (1) UPLOAD key (I pulled it from android/app/nyus-release-key.jks): 7D:B7:33:AB:42:1F:74:C8:7E:D4:07:57:0B:9B:BD:0E:0C:DD:E0:0E:6B:DB:44:71:7D:25:BF:9C:1A:48:9B:5F  (2) PLAY APP SIGNING key SHA-256 — Play Console → your app → Test and release → App integrity → App signing key certificate (THIS is the one that matters for Play installs, since Play re-signs). Also confirm the OAuth consent screen is 'In production' (not 'Testing', which blocks non-allowlisted users). After adding the SHA(s), allow a few minutes and retry — no app rebuild needed. Email OTP sign-in is unaffected.
